title: Getting Started with Angular
content:
### Creating a New Angular Project

Use the Angular CLI to scaffold a new project:

```bash
npm install -g @angular/cli
ng new my-app --standalone false
cd my-app
ng serve
```

### Project Structure

```
my-app/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ src/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ app/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ app.component.ts       # Root component
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ app.component.html
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ app.component.css
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ app.module.ts           # Root module
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ main.ts                     # Application entry point
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ index.html                  # Host HTML page
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ styles.css                  # Global styles
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ angular.json                    # CLI configuration
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ package.json
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ tsconfig.json
```

### Understanding app.module.ts

The `@NgModule` decorator defines the root module:

```typescript
import { NgModule } from '@angular/core';
import { BrowserModule } from '@angular/platform-browser';
import { AppComponent } from './app.component';

@NgModule({
  declarations: [AppComponent],
  imports: [BrowserModule],
  providers: [],
  bootstrap: [AppComponent]
})
export class AppModule { }
```

- **declarations** ‚Äî Components, directives, and pipes that belong to this module.
- **imports** ‚Äî Other modules used by this module (e.g., `BrowserModule`, `FormsModule`).
- **providers** ‚Äî Services registered with the root injector.
- **bootstrap** ‚Äî The root component Angular creates and inserts into `index.html`.

### main.ts ‚Äî The Entry Point

```typescript
import { platformBrowserDynamic } from '@angular/platform-browser-dynamic';
import { AppModule } from './app/app.module';

platformBrowserDynamic().bootstrapModule(AppModule)
  .catch(err => console.error(err));
```

### Key CLI Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `ng serve` | Dev server with live reload |
| `ng generate component foo` | Scaffold a component |
| `ng generate service foo` | Scaffold a service |
| `ng generate module foo` | Scaffold a module |
| `ng build` | Build for production |
| `ng test` | Run unit tests with Karma |
| `ng add` | Add library to project |

quiz.question: Which decorator is used to define an Angular module?
What is the purpose of the `bootstrap` array in NgModule?
options: Specifies the root component to bootstrap
